requirements.title: Limit system access to authorized users, processes, and devices.
Limit system access to authorized transactions and functions.
Control CUI flow according to approved authorizations.
Separate duties to reduce risk of malicious activity.
Apply least privilege, including for privileged functions.
Use non-privileged accounts for nonsecurity functions.
Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions.
Limit unsuccessful logon attempts.
Provide privacy and security notices for system use.
Use session lock with pattern-hiding displays.
Terminate sessions after defined conditions.
Monitor and control remote access sessions.
Use cryptographic mechanisms for remote access confidentiality.
Route remote access through managed access control points.
Authorize remote execution of privileged commands and remote access to security information.
Authorize wireless access before allowing connections.
Protect wireless access with authentication and encryption.
Control connection of mobile devices.
Encrypt CUI on mobile devices and mobile computing platforms.
Verify and control external system connections and use.
Limit use of organizational portable storage on external systems.
Control CUI posted or processed on publicly accessible systems.
Ensure users understand security risks from their activities.
Ensure users are trained for assigned information security duties.
Provide awareness training for insider threat indicators.
Create and retain audit logs for monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting.
Ensure actions of individual users can be uniquely traced.
Review and update logged events.
Alert when audit logging fails.
Correlate audit review, analysis, and reporting processes.
Reduce audit findings into actionable reports.
Provide audit reduction and report generation capability.
Protect audit information and tools from unauthorized access or modification.
Limit audit management functionality to authorized users.
Establish and maintain baseline configurations.
Establish and enforce security configuration settings.
Track, review, approve or disapprove, and log system changes.
Analyze security impact before changes are made.
Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions for changes.
Use least functionality by configuring systems for essential capabilities.
Restrict, disable, or prevent nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services.
Apply deny-by-exception policy for software execution where appropriate.
Control and monitor user-installed software.
Identify system users, processes, and devices.
Authenticate users, processes, and devices before access.
Use multifactor authentication for privileged and non-privileged access.
Use replay-resistant authentication mechanisms.
Prevent reuse of identifiers for a defined period.
Disable identifiers after a defined inactivity period.
Enforce minimum password complexity and change requirements.
Prevent password reuse for a defined number of generations.
Allow temporary password use only for initial logon and require immediate change.
Store and transmit only cryptographically protected passwords.
Obscure authentication feedback.
Establish operational incident-handling capability.
Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials.
Test incident response capability.
Perform maintenance on organizational systems.
Control tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel used for maintenance.
Ensure equipment removed for off-site maintenance is sanitized.
Check media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code.
Require multifactor authentication for nonlocal maintenance sessions.
Supervise maintenance personnel without required access authorization.
Protect system media containing CUI.
Limit access to CUI on system media to authorized users.
Sanitize or destroy media before disposal or reuse.
Mark media with required CUI markings and distribution limitations.
Control access to media during transport outside controlled areas.
Use cryptographic mechanisms to protect CUI on digital media during transport.
Control use of removable media.
Prohibit portable storage devices without identifiable owners.
Protect backup CUI at storage locations.
Limit physical access to systems, equipment, and operating environments.
Protect and monitor physical facilities and infrastructure.
Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity.
Maintain audit logs of physical access.
Control and manage physical access devices.
Enforce safeguarding measures for CUI at alternate work sites.
Screen individuals before authorizing access to systems containing CUI.
Protect CUI during and after personnel actions such as termination or transfer.
Periodically assess risk to operations, assets, and individuals.
Scan for vulnerabilities and remediate according to risk.
Remediate vulnerabilities according to risk assessments.
Periodically assess security controls to determine effectiveness.
Develop and implement plans of action for deficiencies.
Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis.
Develop, document, and periodically update system security plans.
Monitor, control, and protect communications at system boundaries.
Use architectural designs that promote effective information security.
Separate user functionality from system management functionality.
Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer through shared resources.
Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components.
Deny network communications traffic by default and allow by exception.
Prevent split tunneling for remote devices unless securely provisioned.
Use cryptographic mechanisms to protect CUI confidentiality in transit.
Terminate network connections after defined inactivity or session end.
Establish and manage cryptographic keys when cryptography is used.
Employ FIPS-validated cryptography when protecting CUI.
Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices and indicate use.
Control and monitor mobile code.
Control and monitor Voice over Internet Protocol technologies.
Protect authenticity of communications sessions.
Protect confidentiality of CUI at rest.
Identify, report, and correct system flaws in a timely manner.
Provide protection from malicious code.
Monitor security alerts and advisories and take appropriate action.
Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available.
Perform periodic and real-time malicious code scans.
Monitor systems to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.
Identify unauthorized system use.
